# UC-22.14.69 · Network Disconnect for Inactive Sessions on Admin Services

## Description

Measures idle durations on administrative listeners for SC-10 network disconnect controls (proxy perspective).

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes proxy and sourcetypes zscaler:web, zscaler:web`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Validate `transaction` keys; (2) align idle timeout to policy minutes; (3) alert on excessive admin idle tunnels; (4) document VPN vs ZTNA differences. Indexes required: `index=proxy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=zscaler:web`, `sourcetype=zscaler:web``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: F5 BIG-IP APM or Zscaler logs via TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zscaler:web` or `f5:apm:session`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Validate `transaction` keys; (2) align idle timeout to policy minutes; (3) alert on excessive admin idle tunnels; (4) document VPN vs ZTNA differences.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype=zscaler:web earliest=-24h app_class="Admin"
| transaction user maxpause=30m maxspan=24h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,1)
| where idle_mins>240
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Disconnect for Inactive Sessions on Admin Services (SC-10)** — Measures idle durations on administrative listeners for SC-10 network disconnect controls (proxy perspective).

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:web` or `f5:apm:session`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): F5 BIG-IP APM or Zscaler logs via TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: zscaler:web. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype=zscaler:web, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **idle_mins** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where idle_mins>240` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (long idle admin sessions), Timeline

## SPL

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype=zscaler:web earliest=-24h app_class="Admin"
| transaction user maxpause=30m maxspan=24h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,1)
| where idle_mins>240
```

## Visualization

Table (long idle admin sessions), Timeline. Key fields: `idle_mins`.
